primer.markdown:
# Planner plus Executor: the validated shape

A single Claude agent trying to both *decide* what to build and *do* the building is a common first instinct — and a common first failure mode. The Agent Builder Guide (lines 218-227) flags the alternative: split the work. A **Planning Agent** (strategic, 5-10 seconds, 2048-token budget) parses intent, picks templates, and emits a `DesignPlan` as structured JSON. An **Execution Agent** (tactical, 20-25 seconds, 8192-token budget) receives that plan and only has to *follow it*, generating assets and assembling the design via MCP tools.

This is the pattern the guide stars with `⭐ RECOMMENDED` — and the only pattern with a validated quality number: **9.5/10 on the CANVA-CODE run**, the highest step-score in the whole workflow. Total wall time came in around 28 seconds against a 30-second target; token cost settled at about $0.32 per design against a $0.50 ceiling; first-try quality hit 92%; brand consistency hit 99%+. Those are not aspirational figures — they are line-517 of the guide, next to the word *Validated*.

Why does the split work? Three reasons stated in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` lines 74-78: clear separation of concerns (*what* vs *how*), per-role optimization (different system prompts, different token budgets, different tools), and better error recovery (a failed execution does not corrupt the plan). Iterations become cheap — you refine the design without re-planning it. For 3-10 API calls per request, this is the pattern. Fewer calls, go single-agent. More than ten, go multi-agent. In between — which is most real projects — the two-agent pattern is the quiet winner.
primer.markdown_es:
# Planificador más Ejecutor: la forma validada

Un solo agente Claude que intenta a la vez *decidir* qué construir y *construirlo* es un primer instinto común — y una primera forma común de fallar. La Guía del Constructor de Agentes (líneas 218-227) señala la alternativa: separa el trabajo. Un **Agente Planificador** (estratégico, 5-10 segundos, presupuesto de 2048 tokens) interpreta la intención, elige plantillas y emite un `DesignPlan` como JSON estructurado. Un **Agente Ejecutor** (táctico, 20-25 segundos, presupuesto de 8192 tokens) recibe ese plan y sólo tiene que *seguirlo*, generando activos y ensamblando el diseño mediante herramientas MCP.

Este es el patrón que la guía marca con `⭐ RECOMENDADO` — y el único con un número de calidad validado: **9.5/10 en la corrida de CANVA-CODE**, la puntuación más alta del flujo completo. El tiempo total quedó cerca de 28 segundos contra un objetivo de 30; el costo en tokens rondó los $0.32 por diseño contra un techo de $0.50; la calidad al primer intento alcanzó 92%; la consistencia de marca superó el 99%.

¿Por qué funciona la división? Tres razones indicadas en `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` líneas 74-78: separación clara de responsabilidades (*qué* vs *cómo*), optimización por rol (prompts distintos, presupuestos distintos, herramientas distintas) y mejor recuperación ante errores (una ejecución fallida no corrompe el plan).
primer.key_insight: Two agents, two budgets, two jobs. The Planning Agent decides (5-10s, strategic); the Execution Agent builds (20-25s, tactical). Validated at 9.5/10 quality on CANVA-CODE.
primer.key_insight_es: Dos agentes, dos presupuestos, dos tareas. El Planificador decide (5-10s, estratégico); el Ejecutor construye (20-25s, táctico). Validado en calidad 9.5/10 en CANVA-CODE.

visual.alt: A left-to-right flow diagram of the two-agent pattern. On the left, a Planning Agent box labeled 'Strategic · 5-10s · 2048 tokens' with three tool-chip badges (task_decomposition, template_selection, brand_analysis). An arrow labeled 'DesignPlan (JSON Schema)' crosses the center. On the right, an Execution Agent box labeled 'Tactical · 20-25s · 8192 tokens' with three tool-chip badges (PRIMARY_API, SECONDARY_SERVICES, state_manager). Below, a metrics bar shows validated numbers: total 28s, cost $0.32, quality 92%, brand 99%+, score 9.5/10.

sandbox_policy.csp: default-src 'none'; style-src 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; script-src 'unsafe-inline'; img-src data:; font-src https://fonts.gstatic.com; connect-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'self'

interactive.success_criterion: Learner reads the annotated two-agent flow, inspects the Planning Agent's system prompt (lines 343-359) alongside the Execution Agent's system prompt (lines 385-404), and matches each responsibility (parse intent, select template, generate assets, export design) to the correct agent in a static drag-free matching quiz.

artifact.excerpt:
### Performance Characteristics (Validated)

| Metric | Target | Actual (CANVA-CODE) |
|--------|--------|---------------------|
| **Total Time** | < 30s | ~28s ✅ |
| **Planning Time** | 5-10s | ~7s |
| **Execution Time** | 20-25s | ~21s |
| **Token Usage** | 10,000-15,000 | 12,500 avg |
| **Cost per Design** | < $0.50 | $0.32 ✅ |
| **Quality (first try)** | 90%+ | 92% ✅ |
| **Brand Consistency** | 98%+ | 99%+ ✅ |

**Validated on**: CANVA-CODE project (2025-12-15)
**Quality Score**: 9.5/10 (highest in workflow)

questions.prompt: According to the CLAUDE-SDK-AGENT-BUILDER-GUIDE.md decision matrix, when should you choose the two-agent pattern over single-agent or multi-agent?
options: When you have fewer than 3 API calls per request
When you have 3-10 API calls per request
When you have more than 10 API calls per request
questions.explanation: The Agent Architecture Decision Matrix (lines 17-26) maps API-call volume to pattern: `< 3` → Single, `3-10` → Two, `> 10` → Multi. The guide marks the Two-Agent row with the star and the validated 9.5/10 quality score (line 25).

questions.prompt: In the TwoAgentDesignSystem TypeScript example, what `max_tokens` budget does the Planning Agent use versus the Execution Agent?
questions.explanation: Lines 362-363 set the Planning Agent to `max_tokens: 2048` (strategic, short); lines 408-409 set the Execution Agent to `max_tokens: 8192` (tactical, long-running with tool iteration). The smaller planner budget enforces the structural output discipline.

questions.prompt: Per the validated CANVA-CODE numbers in CLAUDE-SDK-AGENT-BUILDER-GUIDE.md lines 512-520, what was the actual cost-per-design and how did it compare to the target?
questions.explanation: Line 520: `Cost per Design | < $0.50 | $0.32 ✅`. The two-agent pattern came in at $0.32 per design, 36% under the $0.50 ceiling. This is one of the reasons the pattern carries the ⭐ RECOMMENDED marker.
